records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 26. It has been also narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Verily the Lord maintains a believer; no forty days pass but that He presents him either with an ailment in his body, or with an affliction in his family and wealth, or any other affliction of the world, that He may thereby reward him for that.
records.arabicText: 26 - وعنه (ع) قال: إن الربَّ ليتعاهدُ المؤمنَ؛ فما يمُرّ به أربعون صباحاً إلاّ تعاهده إمّا بمرضٍ في جسده وإما بمصيبة في أهله وماله أو بمصيبة من مصائب الدّنيا ليؤجِرَه الله عليه.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 26. It has been also narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Verily the Lord maintains a believer; no forty days pass but that He presents him either with an ailment in his body, or with an affliction in his family and wealth, or any other affliction of the world, that He may thereby reward him for that.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 27. It has been narrated that Ibn Hamran said: I heard him (i.e. al-Sadiq (a.s)) say: No forty nights pass for a believer without something befalling him for which he is rewarded - the least of which is an anxiety whose cause he does not understand.
records.arabicText: 27 - عن ابن حمران قال: سمعته يقول: ما مِن مؤمنٍ يمرُّ به أربعون ليلةً إلاّ وقد يُذكر بشئٍ يؤجر عليه، أدناه هَمٌّ لا يدري من أين هو.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 27. It has been narrated that Ibn Hamran said: I heard him (i.e. al-Sadiq (a.s)) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: No forty nights pass for a believer without something befalling him for which he is rewarded - the least of which is an anxiety whose cause he does not understand.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 28. It has been also narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: No forty days pass for a believer without the Glorious and most High Lord presenting him with pain in his body or loss of wealth, or an affliction for which Allah rewards him.
records.arabicText: 28 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) لا يصير على المؤمن أربعون صباحاً إلاّ تعاهده الربُّ تبارك وتعالى بوجَع في جسده أو ذَهاب ماله أو مصيبة يؤجره الله عليها.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 28. It has been also narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: No forty days pass for a believer without the Glorious and most High Lord presenting him with pain in his body or loss of wealth, or an affliction for which Allah rewards him.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 29. It has been also narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: A believer never escapes one of three (problems) if not all three! Either a family member who shuts him out, or a neighbor who troubles him, or a person he meets on his way to work (who troubles him). Even if a believer were (to isolate himself) on a mountain top, Allah would send a devil to bother him. And Allah provides for him (the believer) comfort from his faith.”
records.arabicText: 29 - وعنه (ع) قال ما فَلتَ المؤمنُ من واحدةٍ من ثلاث، أو جمِعتْ عليه الثلاثةُ: أن يكون معه مَن يغلق عليه بابَه في داره، أو جارٍ يؤذيه أو مَن في طريقه إلى حوائجه يؤذيه، ولوأنّ مؤمناً على قلّة جبلٍ لبعث الله شيطاناً يؤذيه، ويجعل الله له من إيمانه أنساً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 29. It has been also narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: A believer never escapes one of three (problems) if not all three! Either a family member who shuts him out, or a neighbor who troubles him, or a person he meets on his way to work (who troubles him). Even if a believer were (to isolate himself) on a mountain top, Allah would send a devil to bother him. And Allah provides for him (the believer) comfort from his faith.”

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 30. It has been narrated that Muhammad ibn Muslim said: I heard Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) saying: No forty nights pass for a believer except a matter befalls him that grieves him and reminds him (of Allah).
records.arabicText: 30 - عن محمد بن مسلم قال: سمعت أبا عبد الله (ع) يقول المؤمنُ لا يمضي عليه أربعون ليلةً إلاّ عُرض له أمرٌ يحزِنه، ويُذكّره به.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 30. It has been narrated that Muhammad ibn Muslim said: I heard Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: No forty nights pass for a believer except a matter befalls him that grieves him and reminds him (of Allah).

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 31. It has been narrated that Abu al-Sabah reported: I was with Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) when a man complained to him saying, 'My son and my brothers have cut themselves off from me and my (fellow Muslim) brothers have (also) alienated themselves from me.' 'Truth (haqq) has a dynasty,' replied Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s), 'and so does falsehood (batil). And in each one of them, the other is disgraced. During the rule of falsehood, the least affliction that befalls a believer is that his sons and brothers break ties with him and his (fellow Muslim) brothers abandon him. And if a believer experiences a life of ease and comfort in the dynasty of falsehood, he will be afflicted in his physical body, wealth or family until Allah purifies him of the luxury that he had acquired in order that his share may thereby be reserved in the dynasty of truth. Therefore be patient and rejoice.”
records.arabicText: 31 - عن أبي الصباح قال: كنت عند أبي عبد الله (ع)، فشكى إليه رجل، فقال: عقني ولدي وأخوتي وجفاني إخواني، فقال أبو عبد الله (ع): إنّ للحقِّ دولةً، وللباطلِ دولة، وكلُّ واحد منهما ذليلٌ في دولة صاحبه. وإنّ أدنى ما يُصيب المؤمنَ في دولة الباطلِ أن يعقّه وِلدُه وإخوتُه ويجفوه إخوانُه. وما من مؤمن يُصيب رفاهيةً في دولة الباطل إلاّ ابتُليَ في بدنه أو ماله أو أهله، حتىّ يخلّصه الله تعالى من السّعةِ التي كان أصابها في دولة الباطل، ليؤخَّرَ به حظّه في دولة الحق، فاصبروا وأبشِروا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 31. It has been narrated that Abu al-Sabah reported: I was with Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) when a man complained to him saying,
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'My son and my brothers have cut themselves off from me and my (fellow Muslim) brothers have (also) alienated themselves from me.' 'Truth (haqq) has a dynasty,' replied Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s), 'and so does falsehood (batil). And in each one of them, the other is disgraced. During the rule of falsehood, the least affliction that befalls a believer is that his sons and brothers break ties with him and his (fellow Muslim) brothers abandon him. And if a believer experiences a life of ease and comfort in the dynasty of falsehood, he will be afflicted in his physical body, wealth or family until Allah purifies him of the luxury that he had acquired in order that his share may thereby be reserved in the dynasty of truth. Therefore be patient and rejoice.”

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 32. It has been narrated that 'Ali ibn Al-Husayn and Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: The soul of a (dead) believer is asked - when it is being washed (for funeral), 'Would it please you to be returned to the body that you resided in?' It would reply, 'What am I to do with (more) afflictions, loss and grief?'
records.arabicText: 32 - عن علي بن الحسين وأبي جعفر (ع) قالا إنّ المؤمنَ ليُقال لروحِه - وهو يُغسّلُ -: أيسُرُّكِ أن تردّي إلى الجسدِ الذي كنتِ فيه؟ فتقول: ما أصنعُ بالبلاء والخسران والغم؟
records.thaqalaynSanad: 32. It has been narrated that 'Ali ibn Al-Husayn and Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The soul of a (dead) believer is asked - when it is being washed (for funeral), 'Would it please you to be returned to the body that you resided in?' It would reply, 'What am I to do with (more) afflictions, loss and grief?'

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 33. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said that the Messenger of Allah (a.s) said: Allah [M.G] says: O World! Visit My believing servant with all kinds of afflictions in what he is preoccupied with in his worldly affairs and strain his means of livelihood and do not be lax with him lest he seeks refuge in you.
records.arabicText: 33 - وعن أبي جعفر (ع) قال: قال رسول الله (ص): يقول الله (جلّ جلاله) يا دُنيا، مُرّي على عبدي المؤمنِ بأنواع البلايا وما هو فيه من أمرِ دنياه وضيّقي عليه في معيشته ولا تحلولي له فيسكن إليك.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 33. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said that the Messenger of Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah [M.G] says: O World! Visit My believing servant with all kinds of afflictions in what he is preoccupied with in his worldly affairs and strain his means of livelihood and do not be lax with him lest he seeks refuge in you.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 34. Al-Sabbah ibn Siyabah narrated: I asked Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s), 'The affliction that befalls a believer is it because of a sin?' 'No,' he replied, 'It is so that He (Allah) may hear his wails and complaints and supplications for which good deeds are recorded for him and misdeeds are wiped out from him, and a treasure is amassed for him for the Day of Resurrection.
records.arabicText: 34 - عن الصباح بن سيابة قال: قلت لأبي عبد الله (ع): ما أصاب المؤمن من بلاء فبذنب؟ قال لا، ولكنْ ليَسمعَ أنينَه وشكواه ودعاءَه الذي يُكتب له بالحسنات وتحُطّ عنه السيئاتُ وتدَّخرُ له يوم القيامة.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 34. Al-Sabbah ibn Siyabah narrated: I asked Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s),
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'The affliction that befalls a believer is it because of a sin?' 'No,' he replied, 'It is so that He (Allah) may hear his wails and complaints and supplications for which good deeds are recorded for him and misdeeds are wiped out from him, and a treasure is amassed for him for the Day of Resurrection.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 35. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Allah [M.G] speaks apologetically to his destitute (believing) servant in this world - like a brother explaining himself to a brother - saying, 'Nay, I swear by My Might and My Glory, I did not impoverish you to disgrace you. Raise this veil and look at what I have given you in compensation for this world.' So, when the veil is removed for him and he sees what Allah has substituted for him in exchange for this world, he will exclaim, 'I have not been harmed, O Lord, seeing what You have given me in exchange.
records.arabicText: 35 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) أنه قال إنَّ الله (جلّ جلاله) لَيعتذرُ إلى عبده المحوج الّذي كان في الدنيا كما يعتذر الأخُ إلى أخيه، فيقول: لا وعزّتي وجلالي ما أفقرتُك لهوانٍ كان بك عليَّ، فارفع هذا الغطاءَ فانظر ما عوّضتُك من الدنيا. فيُكشفُ له فيَنظرُ ما عوّضه الله (جلّ جلاله) من الدنيا، فيقول: ما ضرَّني يا ربُّ مع ما عوَّضتَني.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 35. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah [M.G] speaks apologetically to his destitute (believing) servant in this world - like a brother explaining himself to a brother - saying, 'Nay, I swear by My Might and My Glory, I did not impoverish you to disgrace you. Raise this veil and look at what I have given you in compensation for this world.' So, when the veil is removed for him and he sees what Allah has substituted for him in exchange for this world, he will exclaim, 'I have not been harmed, O Lord, seeing what You have given me in exchange.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 36. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: The best of 'gulps' is anger that is swallowed despite being unbearable; for the greatest reward comes with the greatest affliction. Allah never loved people but that He tried them (with afflictions).
records.arabicText: 36 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) أنه قال نِعمَ الجُرعةُ الغيظ لمَن صَبرَ عَليها، فإنّ عظيمَ الأجر لمَعَ عظيمِ البلاءِ، وما أحبَّ اللهُ قوماً إلاّ ابتَلاهم.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 36. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The best of 'gulps' is anger that is swallowed despite being unbearable; for the greatest reward comes with the greatest affliction. Allah never loved people but that He tried them (with afflictions).

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 37. Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) narrated that the Prophet (a.s) said: Allah [M.G] has said: Amongst My believing servants there are those whose religious affairs are not set right except by wealth, affluence and health; so, I give them these as their lot that the affairs of their religion may be set right.And amongst the servants there are those whose religious affairs are not set right except by hunger, poverty and disease; so, I afflict them with hunger, poverty and disease so that the affairs of their religion may be set right for them.
records.arabicText: 37 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) قال: قال النبي (ص): قال الله (جلّ جلاله) إنّ مِن عبادي المؤمنين لَعباداً لا يصلُح لهم أمرُ دينِهم إلاّ بالغِنى والسّعةِ والصّحة في البدن؛ فأَبلوهُم بالغنى والسّعة والصّحّة في البدن فيصلُح لهم أمرُ دينهم. وقال: إنّ من العباد لَعباداً لا يصلُح لهم أمرُ دينهم الاّ بالفاقةِ والمَسكنةِ والسقم في أبدانهم، فَأبلوهم بالفَقر والفاقة والمسكنةِ والسقم في أبدانهم فيصلح لهم عليه أمرُ دينِهم.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 37. Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) narrated that the Prophet (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah [M.G] has said: Amongst My believing servants there are those whose religious affairs are not set right except by wealth, affluence and health; so, I give them these as their lot that the affairs of their religion may be set right.And amongst the servants there are those whose religious affairs are not set right except by hunger, poverty and disease; so, I afflict them with hunger, poverty and disease so that the affairs of their religion may be set right for them.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 38. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Allah has taken a pledge from the believer that his words will be belied and that he would not be able to avenge himself against his enemy.
records.arabicText: 38 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) قال أخذَ الله ميثاقَ المؤمن ِعلى ألاّ يُصَدّقَ في مقالته ولا يُنتَصَفَ من عَدوّه.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 38. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah has taken a pledge from the believer that his words will be belied and that he would not be able to avenge himself against his enemy.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 39. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: When Allah loves a person, He chokes him with afflictions until he is immersed in them completely and trials flow towards him copiously. Then when he calls out to Him, He (Allah) replies, 'I am here My servant, here I am! If I wished to hasten towards you what you are asking for, then indeed I am able to do it; but what I have amassed for you (for the hereafter) is better for you.'
records.arabicText: 39 - وعن أبي جعفر (ع) قال إنّ الله (جلّ جلاله) إذا أحَبَّ عبداً غثَّه بالبلاء غثّاً وثَجّهُ بالبلاء ثَجّاً، فإذا دعاه قال: لبَّيكَ عبدي، لبّيك عبدي، لئِن عجّلتُ لك ما سألتَ إني على ذلك لقادرٌ، ولئِن ذخَرتُ لك فما أدَّخَرتُ لك خيرٌ لك.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 39. It has been narrated that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: When Allah loves a person, He chokes him with afflictions until he is immersed in them completely and trials flow towards him copiously. Then when he calls out to Him, He (Allah) replies, 'I am here My servant, here I am! If I wished to hasten towards you what you are asking for, then indeed I am able to do it; but what I have amassed for you (for the hereafter) is better for you.'

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 40. It has been narrated that Abu Hamzah said that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: O Thabit! When Allah loves a person, He pours afflictions on him and rains them heavily on him. And (all the while) we pass our days and nights with him (unaware of his suffering).
records.arabicText: 40 - عن أبي حمزة قال أبو عبد الله (ع) يا ثابتُ، إنّ الله إذا أحّبّ عبداً غثّه بالبلاء غثّاً وثَجّه به ثَجّاً، وأنا وإيّاكم لنُصبح به ونمسي.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 40. It has been narrated that Abu Hamzah said that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: O Thabit! When Allah loves a person, He pours afflictions on him and rains them heavily on him. And (all the while) we pass our days and nights with him (unaware of his suffering).

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 41. It is narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: The disciples of 'Isa (Prophet Jesus (a.s)) complained to him about what they were suffering at the hands of others. He said to them, 'Believers have always been despised. Their faith is like a wheat kernel - how sweet its taste is but how severe its treatment (in the process of purification) is!'
records.arabicText: 41 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) قال إنّ الحواريّين شكَوا إلى عيسى ما يلقَونَ من الناسِ وشدّتهم عليهم، فقال: إنّ المؤمنينَ لم يزالوا مبغَضين وإيمانُهم كحَبَّة القَمح ما أحلى مذاقَها وأكثرَ عذابها.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 41. It is narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The disciples of 'Isa (Prophet Jesus (a.s)) complained to him about what they were suffering at the hands of others. He said to them, 'Believers have always been despised. Their faith is like a wheat kernel - how sweet its taste is but how severe its treatment (in the process of purification) is!'

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 42. It has been narrated that 'Abd al-A`la ibn A'yun said: I heard Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) saying: If you intend to be my brothers and my companions, then prepare yourselves for enmity and hatred of people otherwise, you are not my companions.
records.arabicText: 42 - عن عبد الأعلى بن أعين قال: سمعت أبا عبد الله (ع) يقول إنْ أردتُم أنْ تكونوا إخواني وأصحابي فوَطِّنوا أنفسَكم على العَداوة والبغضاء من الناسِ، وإلاّ فلستم لي بأصحابٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 42. It has been narrated that 'Abd al-A`la ibn A'yun said: I heard Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: If you intend to be my brothers and my companions, then prepare yourselves for enmity and hatred of people otherwise, you are not my companions.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 43. Muhammad ibn 'Ajlan reported: I was with my master Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) when a man complained to him (of a need). 'Be patient,' he (a.s) told him, 'for Allah will give you relief.' Then he (al-Sadiq (a.s)) remained silent for a while. Then he turned towards the man and said, 'Tell me about the prison of Al-Kufah, how is it?' 'May Allah protect you,' the man replied, 'It is confining in space, putrid in smell and its inmates are in the worst of conditions.' 'But you are in a prison too,' retorted Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s), 'Do you expect to be at ease? Do you not know that the world is a prison for the believer?'
records.arabicText: 43 - عن محمد بن عجلان قال: كنت عند سيدي أبي عبد الله (ع): فشكى اليه رجل الحاجة، فقال: إصبر فإنّ الله (جلّ جلاله) يجعل لك فرجاً. ثم سكت ساعة، ثم أقبل على الرجل فقال: أخبرني عن سجن الكوفة كيف هو؟ قال: أصلحك الله ضيق منتن وأهله بأسوء حالة. فقال (ع): إنما أنتَ في السجن، تريد أن تكون في سعة. أما علمت أن الدنيا سجن المؤمن؟
records.thaqalaynSanad: 43. Muhammad ibn 'Ajlan reported: I was with my master Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) when a man complained to him (of a need).
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'Be patient,' he (a.s) told him, 'for Allah will give you relief.' Then he (al-Sadiq (a.s)) remained silent for a while. Then he turned towards the man and said, 'Tell me about the prison of Al-Kufah, how is it?' 'May Allah protect you,' the man replied, 'It is confining in space, putrid in smell and its inmates are in the worst of conditions.' 'But you are in a prison too,' retorted Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s), 'Do you expect to be at ease? Do you not know that the world is a prison for the believer?'

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 44. It is narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Verily, when Allah loves a person, He sends him an angel saying, 'Strike him with disease and intensify afflictions on him; and every time he is relieved of one trial, afflict with another that is more severe, and be hard on him - until he starts remembering Me, for I long to hear his supplications and his voice calling out to Me' And when He despises a person, He appoints him an angel and says, 'Keep him healthy and provide for him that he may not remember Me; for I do not wish to hear his voice.'
records.arabicText: 44 - عن أبي عبد الله (ع) قال إنّ اللهَ إذا أحبَّ عبداً بعثَ إليه ملَكاً فيقول: أسقِمْه وشدِّد البَلاء عَليه فإذا برَأ مِن شَئ فابْتَلِه لما هو أشَدُّ منه، وقَوِّ علَيه حتىّ يذكُرَني، فإني أشتهي أن أسمَع دُعاءَ‌ه. وإذا أبغَضَ عبداً وكَّلَ به ملَكاً قال: صَحِّحه وأعطِه كي لا يذكرَني، فإني لا أشتهي أن أسمع صوتَه.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 44. It is narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Verily, when Allah loves a person, He sends him an angel saying, 'Strike him with disease and intensify afflictions on him; and every time he is relieved of one trial, afflict with another that is more severe, and be hard on him - until he starts remembering Me, for I long to hear his supplications and his voice calling out to Me' And when He despises a person, He appoints him an angel and says, 'Keep him healthy and provide for him that he may not remember Me; for I do not wish to hear his voice.'

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 45. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: A person may have a status with his Lord that he cannot attain by his actions. Therefore, he is tried with his body or tried with his possessions, or he is tried with his children. And if he bears patience, Allah raises him to that status.
records.arabicText: 45 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) قال إنّ العبدَ يكون له عِند ربّه درجَةٌ لا يبلُغُها بعَمَله فيُبتلى في جسده أو يُصاب في ماله أو يصاب في ولده، فإنْ هو صَبر بَلَّغَه الله إيّاها.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 45. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: A person may have a status with his Lord that he cannot attain by his actions. Therefore, he is tried with his body or tried with his possessions, or he is tried with his children. And if he bears patience, Allah raises him to that status.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 46. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said that the Prophet (a.s) said: How wonderful the believer is! Whatever Allah decrees is always good for him. When he is afflicted, he bears patience and when he is bestowed, he gives thanks.
records.arabicText: 46 - وعن أبي جعفر (ع) قال: قال رسول الله (ص) عجَباً للمؤمنِ، إنّ الله لا يقضي قضاءً إلاّ كان خيراً له، فإن ابتُليَ صبرَ، وإن أُعطيَ شَكر.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 46. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said that the Prophet (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: How wonderful the believer is! Whatever Allah decrees is always good for him. When he is afflicted, he bears patience and when he is bestowed, he gives thanks.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 47. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said: Allah gives this world to the one He loves as well as the one He despises. But He gives the hereafter only to the one He loves. And if a believer were to ask the Lord for a foot of land in this world, He may deny him; but if he were to ask for the hereafter, He would give him whatever he would wish. He gives the disbeliever whatever he wishes in this world but if he were to ask for a foot of land in the hereafter, He would not give that to him.
records.arabicText: 47 - وعن أبي جعفر (ع) قال إنّ الله (جلّ جلاله) يعطي الدنيا من يحبّ ويُبغض، ولا يعطي الآخرة الاّ من أحبَّ، وإنّ المؤمن ليسأل الربّ موضع سوطٍ في الدنيا فلا يعطيه إيّاه ويسألهُ الآخرةَ فيُعطيه ما شاء، ويُعطي الكافرَ في الدنيا ما شاء ويسأل في الآخرة موضع سوط فلا يُعطيه إيّاه.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 47. It has been reported that Abu Ja'far (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah gives this world to the one He loves as well as the one He despises. But He gives the hereafter only to the one He loves. And if a believer were to ask the Lord for a foot of land in this world, He may deny him; but if he were to ask for the hereafter, He would give him whatever he would wish. He gives the disbeliever whatever he wishes in this world but if he were to ask for a foot of land in the hereafter, He would not give that to him.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 48. It has been related that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Allah [M.G] says: Whatever I divert and keep away from My believing servant, I make it good for him. So, let him be pleased with My decree, and let him bear patience over My trials, and let him give thanks over My blessings - and I shall record him amongst the truthful ones.
records.arabicText: 48 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) قال: قال الله (جلّ جلاله) عبدي المؤمنُ لا أصرفه في شئ الاّ جعلتُ ذلك خيراً له، فلْيرضَ بقضائي وليصبرْ على بلائي وليشكرْ على نعمائي أكتُبْه في الصدّيقين عندي.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 48. It has been related that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Allah [M.G] says: Whatever I divert and keep away from My believing servant, I make it good for him. So, let him be pleased with My decree, and let him bear patience over My trials, and let him give thanks over My blessings - and I shall record him amongst the truthful ones.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 49. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: ' The Messenger of Allah (a.s) laughed once until his teeth were showing, 'Will you not ask me why I am laughing?' he asked. 'Indeed, O Messenger of Allah,' inquired the people. 'I am amazed at a Muslim, whatever Allah decrees for him will always turn out good for him in the end,' he said.
records.arabicText: 49 - وعن أبي عبد الله (ع) قال: ضحك رسول الله (ص) حتى بدت نواجذه، ثم قال: ألا تسألوني عما ضحكت؟ قالوا: بلى يا رسول الله، قال عجِبتُ للمرءِ المسلم أنه ليس من قضاءٍ يقضيه اللهُ له إلاّ كان خيراً له في عاقبةِ أمره.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 49. It has been narrated that Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: '
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (a.s) laughed once until his teeth were showing, 'Will you not ask me why I am laughing?' he asked. 'Indeed, O Messenger of Allah,' inquired the people. 'I am amazed at a Muslim, whatever Allah decrees for him will always turn out good for him in the end,' he said.

records.chapter: The Severity of a Believer’s Afflictions
records.author: Ḥusayn b. Saʿīd al-Ahwāzī
records.englishText: 50. Abu 'Abd Allah (a.s) said: Sometimes, a person has a certain level (reserved for him) with Allah that he does not attain except by one of two characteristics: either by an ailment in his body or by the loss of his assets.
records.arabicText: 50 - وقال أبو عبد الله (ع) إنّه لَيكون للعبدِ منـزلةٌ عند الله (جلّ جلاله) لا يبلغُها إلاّ بإحدى الخِصلتين، إمّا ببليّةٍ في جسمه أو بذهاب مالِه.
